docs.title: Welcome
docs.text:
Welcome to the ViGOR Bioinformatics Short Course, a five-day intensive programme designed to equip researchers, laboratory scientists, epidemiologists, and public health professionals with practical bioinformatics skills for infectious disease surveillance and outbreak response.

The course combines interactive lectures with hands-on practical sessions, guiding participants from the fundamentals of bioinformatics to genome assembly, metagenomics, phylogenetics, and workflow development for pathogen genomics.

docs.title: Learning Outcomes
docs.text:
By the end of this course the course participants will be able to:

  • Explain the role of bioinformatics in outbreak response.
  • Navigate Linux command-line environments.
  • Perform pathogen sequence quality control.
  • Conduct genome assembly and phylogenetics analysis.
  • Analyse metagenomic datasets.
  • Build reproducible bioinformatics workflows.
  • Interpret genomic data for public health decision-making.

docs.title: Target Audiences
docs.text: This course is aimed at Researchers, Laboratory Scientists, Bioinformaticians and Public Health Professionals who are at the frontline of Outbreak response and Genomic surveillance.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
While foundational concepts are introduced throughout the course, participants are expected to have:

  • A basic understanding of molecular biology and genetics
  • A basic experience using linux commandline
  • A laptop meeting the recommended software requirements

Participants who are new to linux commandline are encouraged to complete:

The Carpentries :Introduction to commandline for Genomics

docs.title: Acknowledgements
docs.text:
We gratefully acknowledge the contributions of:

  • The course instructors and facilitators for developing and delivering the training materials.
  • The ViGOR project team for coordinating the training programme.
  • Partner institutions and collaborators for their technical expertise and support.
  • The open-source bioinformatics community, whose software, documentation, and educational resources make this training possible.

Need Help?

Use the navigation menu on the left to access each day's material and additional resources.

ViGOR Bioinformatics Training Resource

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Day 1 introduces the fundamental concepts of bioinformatics and their application to pathogen genomics and outbreak response.

docs.title: Learning Objectives
docs.text:
By the end of Day 1, participants will be able to:

  • Explain the role of bioinformatics in infectious disease surveillance.
  • Navigate the Linux command line.
  • Access High Performance Computing (HPC) environments.
  • Retrieve pathogen genomic data from public databases.
  • Perform basic sequence data preprocessing.

docs.title: Sessions
docs.text: Time Topic Trainers 09:00–10:30 Theory A. Lambisia & A. Kakungu 11:00–13:00 Practical K. Wamae, J. Mavu, P. Jepchirchir, P. Njenga & T Wanjiru
